protocol.input_gain_policy: Manual override of BandLab's suggested input gain. Fixed at 0 so the input level into the chain is exactly the known source level. Preserves the -20/-14/-10 level-dependence probe (accepting the adaptive suggestion would peak-normalize all three to a common level and collapse it).
protocol.intensity_policy: Preset default detent ('Normal' = 50% of range). Held constant across all uploads. Slider spans 0% (dry/min) to 100% (max); 50% is the canonical intended voice.
protocol.output_format: WAV (actual bit depth/sample rate recorded per output by audio_metrics)

auto_input_gain_finding.interpretation: BandLab's suggested input gain is a PEAK normalizer: it pre-conditions every upload to ~-4.5 dBFS peak (fixed headroom), independent of loudness. Implied target peaks across all 8 signals span -4.45 to -4.55 dBFS (mean ~-4.49).
auto_input_gain_finding.discriminator: click_track is sparse (peak -0.45, RMS -44). Peak-target predicts ~-4.1 dB; a loudness-target would instead predict a large positive boost. RESULT: -4.1 (peak-target confirmed; not loudness-aware).
auto_input_gain_finding.status: CONFIRMED on all 8 signals (max error 0.05 dB), including the click_track acid test. Input conditioning is a pure peak normalizer to -4.5 dBFS.

click_track.wav.status: confirmed (ACID TEST PASSED: peak-target, not loudness-aware)

operator_notes: Record the value BandLab SUGGESTS for each signal (then override to 0 before mastering). The suggestion is computed from the input file, so it should be identical across presets for a given signal -- confirm it does not change when you switch presets. Fill suggested_input_gain_db and set status=confirmed as you go.
